name: 7 Days to Die Dedicated Server
description: 7 Days to Die dedicated server, installed via SteamCMD (anonymous).

configFiles.description: Main server settings, written by GameDock on first install (edit via the File manager, then Update files won't overwrite it)
World save data, created after first run

setupFiles.content:
<?xml version="1.0"?>
<ServerSettings>
  <property name="ServerName" value="{{SERVER_NAME}}"/>
  <property name="ServerPassword" value="{{SERVER_PASSWORD}}"/>
  <property name="ServerPort" value="{{GAME_PORT}}"/>
  <property name="ServerVisibility" value="2"/>
  <property name="ServerMaxPlayerCount" value="{{MAX_PLAYERS}}"/>
  <property name="GameWorld" value="RWG"/>
  <property name="WorldGenSeed" value="{{WORLD_SEED}}"/>
  <property name="GameName" value="{{SERVER_NAME}}"/>
  <property name="GameDifficulty" value="2"/>
  <property name="TelnetEnabled" value="true"/>
  <property name="TelnetPort" value="8081"/>
  <property name="TelnetPassword" value="{{ADMIN_PASSWORD}}"/>
  <property name="EACEnabled" value="false"/>
</ServerSettings>

notes: serverconfig.xml is generated once on install from the variables above; edit it directly afterwards for anything not exposed here (Update files won't overwrite your changes). EasyAntiCheat is disabled by default for Linux compatibility. GameDock stops the server with SIGTERM rather than telnet, since telnet is a separate protocol from the process console.
